info.title: Diom API
info.description: Welcome to the Diom API documentation!

get.description: Verify the server is up and running.

post.description: Complete an idempotent request with a response
Abandon an idempotent request (remove lock without saving response)
Creates or updates a msgs namespace with the given name.
Gets a msgs namespace by name.
Publishes messages to a topic within a namespace.

Receives messages from a topic using a consumer group.

Each consumer in the group reads from all partitions. Messages are locked by leases for the
specified duration to prevent duplicate delivery within the same consumer group.

Commits an offset for a consumer group on a specific partition.

The topic must be a partition-level topic (e.g. `ns:my-topic~3`). The offset is the last
successfully processed offset; future receives will start after it.

Configures the number of partitions for a topic.

Partition count can only be increased, never decreased. The default for a new topic is 1.

HTTPBearer.description: HTTP Bearer token passed in the `Authorization` header

ttl.description: TTL in seconds for the cached response
TTL in seconds for the lock/response

oneOf.description: Request was already completed, cached response returned

key.description: Optional partition key. Messages with the same key are routed to the same partition.

retry_after.description: Seconds until enough tokens are available (only present when allowed is false)

max_requests.description: Maximum number of requests allowed within the window

retry_after.description: Seconds until at least one token is available (only present when remaining is 0)

refill_amount.description: Number of tokens to add per refill interval

refill_interval.description: Interval in seconds between refills (minimum 1 second)
